target.title: fix(agents): skip implicit provider discovery when models.mode is 'replace' [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `src/agents/models-config.plan.ts:60-61` unconditionally `await`s `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl(...)` (the provider-discovery scan) before reading `cfg.models?.mode`. When the user has set `models.mode: "replace"` to opt out of discovery, this still runs the full implicit-resolver â wasted work and (in some setups) 70+s of startup latency.
- **Why it matters:** The documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` is that `models.mode = "replace"` uses only the configured providers. Honoring it eliminates the discovery cost for replace-mode users and fixes the timing regression they were filing the issue about.
- **What changed:** Added a 3-line early-return at the top of `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` that short-circuits to `mergeProviders({ implicit: {}, explicit: explicitProviders })` when `cfg.models?.mode === "replace"`. The injected `deps.resolveImplicitProviders` seam used by tests is preserved for merge-mode callers.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** `merge`/`undefined` paths are untouched. `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps` (which also reads `cfg.models?.mode` at L156) is unchanged â its existing logic short-circuits cleanly on the empty `implicit` result. No public type changes. No new exports.

Mirrors the sibling short-circuit at `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118` (`loadPickerModelCatalog` does the same `if (cfg.models?.mode === "replace") return ...` before its catalog load).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

(Closest match: agents config plumbing; no checkbox fits cleanly.)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #66957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` ran the implicit provider resolver even when `models.mode === "replace"`, violating the documented contract and adding 70+s of startup latency in replace-mode setups.
- **Real environment tested:** local clone built and tested on macOS arm64 (Node v24.7.0, pnpm 11.1.0, vitest 4.1.6).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /private/tmp/openclaw-triage-20pr/repo

   Test Files  2 passed (2)        # new test file (under 2 project lanes)
        Tests  6 passed (6)         # 3 scenarios Ã 2 lanes
   Test Files  6 passed (6)         # existing models-config tests
        Tests  46 passed (46)
  ```
  The new test file `models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` has 3 scenarios:
  1. `skips implicit discovery when models.mode === 'replace'` â DI spy assertion `not.toHaveBeenCalled`, result contains only explicit providers.
  2. `still resolves implicit when models.mode === 'merge'` â spy called once, result merges both.
  3. `still resolves implicit when models.mode is undefined (defaults to merge)` â spy called once.
  Regression: all 46 existing tests across `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`, `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`, and `models-config.merge.test.ts` continue to pass â they use `mode: undefined` or `merge`, so they enter the same code path as before.
- **Observed result after fix:** all 52 tests pass. With `mode: "replace"` and an injected spy, `resolveImplicitProviders` is never called.
- **What was not tested:** I did not benchmark the wall-clock startup latency improvement in a real OpenClaw setup against the reporter's specific provider configuration (the issue body cites ~72s of `pnpm tsx` cold-load time). The new test proves the implicit-resolver call is genuinely skipped; converting that skip into measured-on-host latency win would require a configured agent with provider-discovery providers loaded.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** on unpatched main, the new test 1 fails â `not.toHaveBeenCalled` is violated because `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl` is awaited unconditionally before the mode is read.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** the function reads `explicitProviders` first, then unconditionally awaits the implicit resolver, then merges. There is no mode check until `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps:156` â too late to skip the discovery work.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` ("`replace` uses only your configured providers") was not enforced anywhere in `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps`. The new test locks the contract via spy assertion.
- **Contributing context (if known):** PR #73557 attempted a similar shape and was closed without merge. The shape used here mirrors the sibling pattern in `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`, which is the canonical in-repo idiom for replace-mode short-circuits.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` (new).
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** with `cfg.models.mode === "replace"`, the injected `resolveImplicitProviders` spy must never be called and the result must contain only the explicit-provider entries.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** uses the same DI seam (`deps.resolveImplicitProviders`) the codebase already uses for testing the merge path. No new mocking infrastructure required.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** none â `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts` and `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts` exercise the merge/undefined paths only.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For users with `models.mode: "replace"`:
- Implicit provider discovery no longer runs. This is the documented contract.
- Startup time drops by the cost of the discovery scan (reported as 70+s in some setups; varies by provider count and `pnpm tsx` cold-load behavior).
- The final `models.json` contents are identical to what the merge-mode emitter would produce given zero implicit providers â i.e. the `providers` map is exactly the user's `cfg.models.providers`.

No change for `merge`/`undefined` mode users.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude Code, Opus 4.7). The fix shape was selected after a 5-lens design debate (MVD, defensive, pattern-match, performance, DX) â all five lenses converged on the same 3-line early-return mirroring `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`. The chosen variant is the smallest viable diff that honors the documented contract.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server on `127.0.0.1:19245`, started OpenClaw Gateway on `127.0.0.1:19246`, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-78441 OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json   --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent   --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].'   --timeout 90
```

The mock model returned a real `sessions_spawn` function call from the parent request with `toolsAllow:["read"]`; the Gateway run accepted it, spawned the child, and the child model request was logged from the same mock provider.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent.

```json
{
  "head": "8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "ba926aed-4c88-41bf-b00e-166c45c58f5a",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --testNamePattern toolsAllow --reporter=dot
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  8 passed | 300 skipped (308)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  30 passed | 488 skipped (518)

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts` - 5 files, 182 tests passed
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard-status-persistence.e2e.test.ts` - 2 files, 2 tests passed
- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Workboard operations view layout, filters, health badges, tooltip-safe card actions, custom dropdowns, edit/create modals, drag/reload flow, read-only behavior, guarded auto-refresh, diagnostics refresh on writable tab load, and the lint-clean follow-up after the Phase 1 polish changes.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw branch rebased onto upstream main at `6e7b23d1b00d241786fc9c5fef20f264d501450c`, using the real local Workboard plugin state plus deterministic Chromium Control UI E2E Workboard flows.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm tsgo:test:ui
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm lint --threads=8
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm ui:i18n:check
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/workboard-status-persistence.e2e.test.ts
PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI before the final rebase-fix commits:

target.title: fix: add clear separator between metadata and user message
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a user message is passed to the LLM, the metadata blocks (sender info, conversation info, etc.) and the actual user message content are joined with only double newlines. This causes the LLM to sometimes misinterpret the message structure, treating the metadata as the entire message and ignoring the actual user content ("message is empty" replies).
- **Why it matters:** Users intermittently get incorrect "message is empty" responses even though their message was correctly stored in session history.
- **What changed:**
  - `get-reply-run.ts`: When inbound metadata is present, inject `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator instead of joining with `\n\n`.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.ts`: Add `stripUserMessageSeparator()` post-processing in both `stripInboundMetadata` (webchat/API/gateway) and `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` (TUI) to remove the separator after metadata blocks are stripped. Gated on `didStripMetadata` flag for defense-in-depth.
  - `strip-inbound-meta.test.ts`: 7 new tests covering separator stripping and legacy preservation.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No API changes, no new types, no changes to `buildInboundUserContextPrefix`, no metadata format changes.

### Why `---openclaw:user-msg---` instead of `---\n**User Message:**`

The separator is a program-generated marker that **cannot exist** in any legacy stored message or user-typed content. This eliminates the need for complex detection logic (`_sep` markers, `seenUserContent` flags, structural JSON parsing) and makes legacy message compatibility trivial: the stripper only removes `---openclaw:user-msg---`, which legacy data never contains.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- LLM now receives a clear `---openclaw:user-msg---` separator between metadata and user content, reducing "message is empty" false positives.
- The separator is stripped in all user-facing surfaces (TUI, webchat, gateway API, session cost logs).
- Legacy messages (pre-upgrade) are completely unaffected â the unique marker cannot appear in historical data.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04)
- Runtime: Node v22.22.1, pnpm 10.23.0

### Steps

1. Send a message to the LLM through any channel with metadata (group chat, sender info, etc.)
2. Observe the prompt construction in `get-reply-run.ts`
3. Verify the separator appears between metadata and user content
4. Verify `stripInboundMetadata` / `stripLeadingInboundMetadata` removes the separator in user-facing views

### Expected

- LLM correctly distinguishes metadata from user message content
- No separator visible in TUI/webchat/gateway/session logs

### Actual

- Confirmed working as expected in unit tests

## Evidence

- [x] Passing tests: 27 strip-inbound-meta tests pass (7 new separator-specific tests)
- New tests cover: separator after single/multiple metadata blocks, no-metadata preservation, non-separator `---` preservation, sentinel-without-metadata-block edge case, legacy message preservation

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: metadata with separator injection, separator stripping after single/multiple metadata blocks, preservation when no metadata present, preservation of `---` in non-separator context
- Edge cases checked: legacy messages with `---\n**User Message:**` user content (preserved intact), sentinel text without metadata fences, user content starting with generic `---`
- What you did **not** verify: Live end-to-end with real LLM provider

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` â legacy messages are unaffected; the unique marker cannot appear in pre-upgrade data
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit
- Files/config to restore: `get-reply-run.ts`, `strip-inbound-meta.ts`
- Known bad symptoms: `---openclaw:user-msg---` appearing in TUI/webchat/session history

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: LLM treats `---openclaw:user-msg---` as confusing noise
  - Mitigation: The marker reads like a boundary tag; LLMs routinely handle similar delimiters in system/user prompts. It's a single line and less disruptive than the metadata blocks themselves.

target.title: fix #89217: Surface approval delivery failures
target.body:
Fixes #89217.

## Summary
- Track explicit exec/plugin approval delivery attempts separately from successful delivery.
- Surface configured delivery failures as no-route approval responses instead of silently falling back to turn-source routing.
- Add regression coverage for shared approval routing, gateway handler behavior, forwarder all-target failure/suppression handling, and iOS push attempted/delivered accounting.

## Root Cause
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause gateway approval delivery failure handling bug fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch stays inside the approval request delivery path and preserves existing fallback behavior only when no explicit delivery route was attempted.
- **Root cause:** The approval request flow only knew whether a delivery succeeded. A configured explicit target that failed delivery was indistinguishable from âno explicit route attemptedâ, so the handler could fall back to turn-source routing and leave the approval waiting silently.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The shared approval source of truth now carries both `delivered` and `attempted`; turn-source fallback is only allowed when no explicit delivery was attempted. Forwarders now throw when every configured target was attempted but produced zero visible deliveries, including failed and suppressed sends, and gateway handlers convert that into an attempted failed delivery.
- **Patch quality notes:** The diff is limited to exec/plugin approval delivery and focused regression tests; no public protocol/schema/default config changes.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Configured exec approval delivery failures now surface immediately instead of being treated like an undelivered optional route that can fall back to turn-source waiting.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local checkout on current `origin/main`, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, production TypeScript modules loaded through `node --import tsx`, with an isolated OpenClaw config file.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/proof-openclaw-config.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/proof-state \
  OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/local-approval-delivery-proof.mjs
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```json
{
  "issue": 89217,
  "proof": "production gateway approval delivery failure behavior",
  "scenarios": {
    "sharedFallbackComparison": {
      "fallbackWithoutExplicitAttempt": {
        "firstResponse": "accepted",
        "remainedPendingBeforeDecision": true,
        "finalDecision": "deny"
      },
      "explicitAttemptFailure": {
        "responseDecision": null,
        "resolvedBy": "no-approval-route",
        "finalResponseCount": 1
      }
    },
    "gatewayHandlerFailure": {
      "responseDecision": null,
      "resolvedBy": "no-approval-route",
      "loggedErrorPrefix": "exec approvals: forward request failed"
    },
    "forwarderAllTargetFailure": {
      "attemptedTargets": 1,
      "failedTarget": {
        "channel": "slack",
        "to": "U-proof",
        "count": 1
      },
      "surfacedError": "exec approvals: failed to deliver request proof-forwarder-request"
    },
    "forwarderAllTargetSuppressed": {
      "attemptedTargets": 1,
      "suppressedTarget": {
        "channel": "slack",
        "to": "U-suppressed",
        "count": 1
      },
      "surfacedError": "exec approvals: failed to deliver request proof-forwarder-suppressed-request"
    }
  },
  "result": "passed"
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** A turn-source fallback remains pending when no explicit delivery was attempted, but an explicit attempted failure resolves the approval with `decision: null` and `resolvedBy: "no-approval-route"`; the production forwarder rejects when all configured targets fail or are suppressed before producing a visible message.
- **What was not tested:** No live Slack/Discord/Telegram/iOS APNs delivery was sent; the proof injects a local failed delivery result at the durable-message boundary to exercise production gateway/forwarder behavior without external channel credentials.

## Tests
- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts`, `src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit attempted failures expire no-route instead of falling back; all-target forwarder failures and suppressed sends throw; partial success remains accepted; iOS attempted/no-device accounting is surfaced.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** These tests pin the shared handler, concrete gateway methods, forwarder, and iOS delivery adapter at the boundaries where the attempted/delivered distinction is consumed.
- **Exact commands run:**
```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts --reporter=dot
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 check:changed
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 diff --check
```
- **Observed test result after fix:** Targeted Vitest passed 9 files / 288 tests; `oxfmt --check` passed on all 10 changed files; `oxlint --deny-warnings` passed on all changed files; `git diff --check` passed; local child-mode `check:changed` passed core lanes including core typecheck, core test typecheck, core lint, import-cycle guard, dependency guards, and changed-file architecture checks.

## Review findings addressed
- **RF-001 status: waiting on author:** Addressed by the focused suppressed-send repair below; expected to clear after bot re-review because the PR now includes the requested code/test/proof update.
- **RF-002 suppressed delivery classification:** Fixed. Durable `status: "suppressed"` now counts as `attempted: true` and `delivered: false`, and all-target suppressed delivery has a regression test.
- **RF-003 compatibility-sensitive fallback change:** Disclosed and bounded. The intentional behavior change still applies only after an explicit configured delivery route was attempted; the no-explicit-route turn-source fallback remains unchanged.
- **RF-004 suppressed-send silent pending gap:** Fixed. A configured target that suppresses every visible approval prompt now surfaces the same delivery failure path instead of leaving the approval pending.
- **RF-005 focused repair shape:** Fixed with a narrow forwarder-only code change plus adjacent test coverage; no API, schema, config default, or channel adapter expansion.
- **RF-006 `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts:407`:** Fixed by classifying suppressed sends before the generic delivered fall-through and by throwing when attempted delivery produces zero visible deliveries.
- **RF-007 requested Vitest coverage:** Fixed. Targeted approval suite passed 9 files / 288 tests in the PR repair worktree.
- **RF-008 whitespace check:** Fixed. `git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 diff --check` passed with no output.

## Risk / Scope
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Explicit approval targets that fail delivery no longer leave operator commands waiting silently on an unintended fallback path.
- **What did NOT change:** No approval protocol schema, public config shape, default approval mode, decision enum, command execution semantics, or live channel credentials changed.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The attempted/delivered decision is handled in shared approval routing and concrete delivery adapters, not downstream string matching or per-channel special cases.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: ðĻ message-delivery`, `merge-risk: ðĻ availability`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes approval request routing when explicit delivery fails, but only after a configured delivery path was actually attempted and failed.
- **Why acceptable:** The old fallback behavior remains intact for the âno explicit route attemptedâ case, while failed configured routes are surfaced so callers do not wait silently.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification

- `PATH="/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
## Real behavior proof

target.title: fix #89217: Surface approval delivery failures
target.body:
Fixes #89217.

## Summary
- Track explicit exec/plugin approval delivery attempts separately from successful delivery.
- Surface configured delivery failures as no-route approval responses instead of silently falling back to turn-source routing.
- Add regression coverage for shared approval routing, gateway handler behavior, forwarder all-target failure/suppression handling, and iOS push attempted/delivered accounting.

## Root Cause
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause gateway approval delivery failure handling bug fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch stays inside the approval request delivery path and preserves existing fallback behavior only when no explicit delivery route was attempted.
- **Root cause:** The approval request flow only knew whether a delivery succeeded. A configured explicit target that failed delivery was indistinguishable from âno explicit route attemptedâ, so the handler could fall back to turn-source routing and leave the approval waiting silently.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The shared approval source of truth now carries both `delivered` and `attempted`; turn-source fallback is only allowed when no explicit delivery was attempted. Forwarders now throw when every configured target was attempted but produced zero visible deliveries, including failed and suppressed sends, and gateway handlers convert that into an attempted failed delivery.
- **Patch quality notes:** The diff is limited to exec/plugin approval delivery and focused regression tests; no public protocol/schema/default config changes.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Configured exec approval delivery failures now surface immediately instead of being treated like an undelivered optional route that can fall back to turn-source waiting.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local checkout on current `origin/main`, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, production TypeScript modules loaded through `node --import tsx`, with an isolated OpenClaw config file.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/proof-openclaw-config.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/proof-state \
  OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91481-evidence/local-approval-delivery-proof.mjs
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```json
{
  "issue": 89217,
  "proof": "production gateway approval delivery failure behavior",
  "scenarios": {
    "sharedFallbackComparison": {
      "fallbackWithoutExplicitAttempt": {
        "firstResponse": "accepted",
        "remainedPendingBeforeDecision": true,
        "finalDecision": "deny"
      },
      "explicitAttemptFailure": {
        "responseDecision": null,
        "resolvedBy": "no-approval-route",
        "finalResponseCount": 1
      }
    },
    "gatewayHandlerFailure": {
      "responseDecision": null,
      "resolvedBy": "no-approval-route",
      "loggedErrorPrefix": "exec approvals: forward request failed"
    },
    "forwarderAllTargetFailure": {
      "attemptedTargets": 1,
      "failedTarget": {
        "channel": "slack",
        "to": "U-proof",
        "count": 1
      },
      "surfacedError": "exec approvals: failed to deliver request proof-forwarder-request"
    },
    "forwarderAllTargetSuppressed": {
      "attemptedTargets": 1,
      "suppressedTarget": {
        "channel": "slack",
        "to": "U-suppressed",
        "count": 1
      },
      "surfacedError": "exec approvals: failed to deliver request proof-forwarder-suppressed-request"
    }
  },
  "result": "passed"
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** A turn-source fallback remains pending when no explicit delivery was attempted, but an explicit attempted failure resolves the approval with `decision: null` and `resolvedBy: "no-approval-route"`; the production forwarder rejects when all configured targets fail or are suppressed before producing a visible message.
- **What was not tested:** No live Slack/Discord/Telegram/iOS APNs delivery was sent; the proof injects a local failed delivery result at the durable-message boundary to exercise production gateway/forwarder behavior without external channel credentials.

## Tests
- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts`, `src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit attempted failures expire no-route instead of falling back; all-target forwarder failures and suppressed sends throw; partial success remains accepted; iOS attempted/no-device accounting is surfaced.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** These tests pin the shared handler, concrete gateway methods, forwarder, and iOS delivery adapter at the boundaries where the attempted/delivered distinction is consumed.
- **Exact commands run:**
```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts --reporter=dot
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.test.ts src/gateway/exec-approval-ios-push.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 check:changed
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 diff --check
```
- **Observed test result after fix:** Targeted Vitest passed 9 files / 288 tests; `oxfmt --check` passed on all 10 changed files; `oxlint --deny-warnings` passed on all changed files; `git diff --check` passed; local child-mode `check:changed` passed core lanes including core typecheck, core test typecheck, core lint, import-cycle guard, dependency guards, and changed-file architecture checks.

## Review findings addressed
- **RF-001 status: waiting on author:** Addressed by the focused suppressed-send repair below; expected to clear after bot re-review because the PR now includes the requested code/test/proof update.
- **RF-002 suppressed delivery classification:** Fixed. Durable `status: "suppressed"` now counts as `attempted: true` and `delivered: false`, and all-target suppressed delivery has a regression test.
- **RF-003 compatibility-sensitive fallback change:** Disclosed and bounded. The intentional behavior change still applies only after an explicit configured delivery route was attempted; the no-explicit-route turn-source fallback remains unchanged.
- **RF-004 suppressed-send silent pending gap:** Fixed. A configured target that suppresses every visible approval prompt now surfaces the same delivery failure path instead of leaving the approval pending.
- **RF-005 focused repair shape:** Fixed with a narrow forwarder-only code change plus adjacent test coverage; no API, schema, config default, or channel adapter expansion.
- **RF-006 `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.ts:407`:** Fixed by classifying suppressed sends before the generic delivered fall-through and by throwing when attempted delivery produces zero visible deliveries.
- **RF-007 requested Vitest coverage:** Fixed. Targeted approval suite passed 9 files / 288 tests in the PR repair worktree.
- **RF-008 whitespace check:** Fixed. `git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91481 diff --check` passed with no output.

## Risk / Scope
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Explicit approval targets that fail delivery no longer leave operator commands waiting silently on an unintended fallback path.
- **What did NOT change:** No approval protocol schema, public config shape, default approval mode, decision enum, command execution semantics, or live channel credentials changed.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The attempted/delivered decision is handled in shared approval routing and concrete delivery adapters, not downstream string matching or per-channel special cases.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: ðĻ message-delivery`, `merge-risk: ðĻ availability`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes approval request routing when explicit delivery fails, but only after a configured delivery path was actually attempted and failed.
- **Why acceptable:** The old fallback behavior remains intact for the âno explicit route attemptedâ case, while failed configured routes are surfaced so callers do not wait silently.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): accept apply op aliases
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90303 â the `wiki_apply` tool schema only accepted the internal op names `create_synthesis` / `update_metadata`, but the CLI and docs use `synthesis` / `metadata`, so those documented calls failed schema validation.

- Accept `synthesis` / `metadata` as `op` aliases in the `wiki_apply` schema (`tool.ts`).
- Normalize the aliases to the internal op names before mutation validation (`apply.ts`), so `synthesis` follows the create-synthesis path and `metadata` the update-metadata path.
- Add focused regression tests for both aliases.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed:** `wiki_apply` rejected the documented CLI op names â `{"op":"synthesis", ...}` / `{"op":"metadata", ...}` failed schema validation (`op must be equal to constant`) because the schema and normalizer only knew `create_synthesis` / `update_metadata` (#90303). After this change both aliases are accepted and normalized to the internal ops.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw source checkout on macOS (arm64), Node v26, pnpm 11.2.2, branch `tui-90303-wiki-apply-aliases`. target.title: refactor(memory): drop redundant agent-id scoping from qmd collection names
target.body:
## Summary

- **What:** drop the redundant `-<agentId>` scoping from managed qmd collection names. Each agent has its **own isolated qmd index** (`agents/<agentId>/qmd/index.sqlite`), so collection names can't collide across agents â the agent-id suffix adds nothing but hardcodes the agent id into collection identifiers.
- **How:** remove `scopeCollectionBase` (and two now-dead path helpers, `isPathInsideRoot` / `canonicalizePathForContainment`); name default collections by their base (`memory-root`, `memory-dir`), the session collection `sessions`, and extra collections by their explicit name. Reverse the legacy migration to **scopedâunscoped** (`migrateLegacyScopedCollections` / `deriveLegacyScopedName`) so existing per-agent indexes migrate cleanly.
- **Why migration is cheap:** qmd collections are a **rebuildable derived index** (not user data), so the migration drops the stale scoped collection and lets the unscoped one be (re)created + reindexed â no import, no data loss (consistent with this repo's "prefer delete/drop/rebuild over import for cache").
- **Outcome:** stable, agent-agnostic collection names; net **â42 LOC** of production code for the de-scope itself (removes `scopeCollectionBase` + two now-dead path helpers in `backend-config.ts`).
- **Orphan cleanup:** the scopedâunscoped migration is **kind-gated**. Engine-owned collections (`memory`/`sessions`, whose base names are engine-generated and not user-suppliable) are removed even when their pattern has drifted from the current default; user-named (`custom`) collections keep the path+pattern guards and are **never** auto-deleted. Without this, a pattern-drifted legacy engine collection lingered as an orphan and was re-walked + re-embedded by every `qmd update` â a recurring reindex cost the migration otherwise shipped. (Trade-off, stated for transparency: for engine kinds this intentionally drops the prior "a user coincidentally named a collection like a legacy scoped name" guard â safe because those base names cannot be user-supplied; `custom` retains it.)
- **Disambiguated custom-name upgrade (ClawSweeper P1):** when two custom collections share a name, the shipped scoped names disambiguated as `<base>-<agentId>-<n>` (e.g. `notes-main`, `notes-main-2`), but the unscoped probe `deriveLegacyScopedName(<base>-<n>)` is `<base>-<n>-<agentId>` (`notes-2-main`) â so `migrateLegacyScopedCollections` missed the second orphan, and qmd then rejected the new unscoped collection on the same path+pattern, stranding it under the legacy name. `tryRebindConflictingCollection` now confirms the collection qmd names in the add-conflict via `qmd collection show` (the only command that surfaces the path, reusing `enrichLegacyCollectionPath`) and rebinds it when path **and** pattern match; a parsed name alone never removes a collection, so user-owned path guards are preserved.
- **Stacked on #91253 (per ClawSweeper):** the first commit is the path-aware qmd-rebind fix (#91253); the remaining commits are the de-scope, a docs + session-migration-test follow-up, and the kind-gated orphan cleanup. The rebind is **required** for the migration to be safe â without path-aware metadata, an upgraded index holding both a stale *unscoped* collection (old path) and a current *scoped* one could keep the stale unscoped target active; with the rebind folded in, the stale unscoped collection is rebound to the current path. Once #91253 lands, this rebases to drop that first commit.

## Linked context

Closes #91259

Depends on / folds in #91253 (path-aware qmd collection rebind) â the first commit of this PR.

Related: #91265 (an earlier PR toward the same proposal; this one folds in the path-aware rebind so existing scoped indexes migrate safely, adds an upgrade-case regression, and includes live before/after behavior proof).

Requested by a maintainer or owner? No â follows from the proposal in #91259.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: managed qmd collection names dropping the redundant `-<agentId>` suffix; existing per-agent indexes migrate scopedâunscoped; and orphaned engine-owned legacy collections are cleaned up.

Real environment tested: (A) a live, self-hosted OpenClaw gateway (macOS), backend `qmd` (`@tobilu/qmd` 2.5.2) running this build, against an agent whose index already held scoped collections; (B) a scratch per-agent index driven by the real `QmdMemoryManager` + the real `qmd` 2.5.2 binary for the orphan-cleanup case; (C) a scratch in-containerâhost rebind driven by the real `QmdMemoryManager` + real `qmd` 2.5.2 binary (the sandbox-mode transition).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
# (A) live gateway â de-scope migration on an agent with existing scoped collections:
openclaw gateway restart
openclaw memory index --force --agent <agent>
sqlite3 <agent-qmd-index>.sqlite "select name,path from store_collections order by name;"

# (B) scratch index â seed two legacy scoped orphans (drifted pattern), run the engine (cli mode):
qmd collection add <ws>/memory --name memory-dir-<agent> --mask "**/*.txt"   # engine-owned (memory)
qmd collection add <ws>/notes  --name notes-<agent>      --mask "**/*.txt"   # user-owned   (custom)
# then QmdMemoryManager.create({ mode: "cli" }) runs the migration; list collections before/after.

# (C) sandbox-mode transition â seed a collection at a stale in-container root, run the engine:
qmd collection add <root>/home/node/.openclaw/teams/x/workspace --name workspace --mask "**/*.md"
# then QmdMemoryManager.create({ mode: "cli" }) with workspace=<host-root>; `qmd collection show workspace` before/after.
```

Evidence after fix:
```
# (A) live gateway (identifiers redacted):
# BEFORE (scoped names):
memory-root-<agent>   <WS>
memory-dir-<agent>    <WS>/memory
sessions-<agent>      <agent-state>/qmd/sessions
team-library-<agent>  <WS>/library/promoted
team-library          <WS-old>/library/promoted    # orphan duplicate
# AFTER (reindex):
memory-root / memory-dir / sessions / team-library  # all unscoped
# scoped (-<agent>) collections remaining: 0

# (B) scratch index, real qmd 2.5.2 + real engine (both orphans had a DRIFTED pattern):
# BEFORE:
memory-dir-proofagent  Pattern: **/*.txt   # engine-owned (memory)
notes-proofagent       Pattern: **/*.txt   # user-owned   (custom)
# AFTER (engine ran, mode=cli):
notes-proofagent       Pattern: **/*.txt   # LEFT  â user data never auto-deleted
memory-root / memory-dir / notes           # unscoped, engine-created from scratch
# memory-dir-proofagent : 1 -> 0  (engine-owned, removed)
# notes-proofagent      : 1 -> 1  (user-owned, preserved)

# (C) sandbox-mode transition, real qmd 2.5.2 + real engine:
# BEFORE: workspace -> .../home/node/.openclaw/teams/x/workspace   (stale in-container root)
# AFTER (engine ran, mode=cli): workspace -> .../host/workspace    (rebound to host root)
```

Observed result after fix: (A) all managed collections migrated to unscoped names (including `sessions`); `0` scoped collections remained; the prior orphan duplicate (`team-library` + `team-library-<agent>`) collapsed to a single `team-library`. (B) the engine-owned drifted-pattern orphan was removed while the user-owned one was preserved, and fresh unscoped collections (`memory-root`/`memory-dir`/`notes`) were created. (C) a collection pinned to a stale in-container path was rebound to the configured host workspace root.

What was not tested: launching an actual Docker sandbox container â the bind-mount + container-launch plumbing is OpenClaw platform infrastructure, outside this PR's diff. The in-containerâhost path transition this PR's code performs is proven with the real binary in (C) above and by a unit test (containerâhost rebind transition, #91253).

Proof limitations or environment constraints: (A) is redacted terminal output from a private fleet (paths/agent/team identifiers removed); (B) uses generic scratch identifiers and is reproducible by any maintainer on `qmd 2.5.2`.

Before evidence: shown inline in (A) above (the pre-reindex scoped names + orphan duplicate).

## Tests and validation

Commands run (clean checkout off `openclaw/openclaw` `main`, re-verified on the current head after the disambiguation fix):
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory` â **444 passed (40 files)**
- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk` â **38 passed (9 files)**
- `pnpm check:test-types` (tsgo) â **clean, 0 errors**
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on the touched files â clean.

Regression coverage:
- Default + custom collection-name tests now assert the unscoped form.
- The unscopedâscoped migration tests were replaced with scopedâunscoped ones.
- **Upgrade-case regression (addresses the flagged compatibility risk):** an index holding BOTH a stale *unscoped* collection (old path) AND a current *scoped* one at the name switch â asserts the stale unscoped collection is rebound to the **current** path (via the folded-in `collection show` path enrichment), not left active.
- **Kind-gated orphan cleanup:** a rewritten migration-guard test â a `memory`-kind legacy scoped collection with a drifted pattern is now **removed** (genuinely fails against the pre-fix guard), while a `custom`-kind one with a drifted pattern is **left** â plus a contract test asserting every managed collection carries a `kind` (the safety gate depends on it).
- **Disambiguated custom-name upgrade regression:** two custom collections both named `notes` at different roots, with legacy `notes-<agentId>` / `notes-<agentId>-2` seeded in a real-`qmd`-shaped mock (name-only `collection list`, path-bearing `collection show`) â asserts the disambiguated orphan (`notes-<agentId>-2`) is removed and both unscoped collections (`notes`, `notes-2`) bind to their correct roots. Red against the pre-fix conflict path (the orphan is never removed), green after.

Multi-agent note: safe by construction â each agent has its own isolated qmd index, so unscoped names cannot collide across agents.

## Risk checklist

User-visible behavior change? Internal only â qmd collection identifiers (a derived index) change; no user-facing API/config change.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Migration only: existing per-agent indexes migrate scopedâunscoped on the next `ensureCollections()` (drop stale scoped + recreate unscoped + reindex). No config keys changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.

Highest-risk area: the scopedâunscoped migration on existing indexes, and the kind-gated removal of legacy scoped collections.

How is that risk mitigated: removal only ever targets the exact derived legacy name `<base>-<agentId>` for a **desired** collection. For `custom` collections the path+pattern guards remain, so a user-named collection is never auto-deleted. For `memory`/`sessions` the base names are engine-generated and cannot be user-supplied, so the derived legacy name is unambiguously engine-owned; if such a collection is still configured it is simply re-added at the same path/pattern (net-zero, a reindex). qmd collections are rebuildable, so any dropped-then-recreated collection reindexes from source with no user-visible data loss.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

What is still waiting: CI + review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: ClawSweeper review findings on #91274 (docs example, session-migration coverage, scoped-duplicate upgrade case, orphan cleanup, and the disambiguated custom-name migration P1 â fixed via the show-verified conflict rebind).

## Summary

- **Problem**: Running cron tasks appear in the task ledger but cannot be cancelled. The command returns a generic message with no operator guidance. Issue #90630.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** â the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token â mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   â mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity â it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.
